The passion
for the craft
philosophy.kanji_caption: The kanji represents deep love and affection for someone or something
philosophy.p1: Not fleeting trends, but deep respect for the metaphor and the core idea. Your unique vision becomes a lasting masterpiece — precisely etched with skill and passion
philosophy.p2: In Irezumi, every line is drawn with intent — driven by deep love for the tradition and devotion to the client's vision. For over five years I have lived this passion in large-scale, traditional Japanese tattoo projects in the heart of Munich

motifs.heading: Japanese tattoo motifs in Munich
motifs.body: Japanese tattoo speaks the language of symbols. Here are the meanings of the most popular motifs Kisha works with in Munich
motifs.kanji_caption: A symbol of inner strength and discipline

dragon.name: Dragon
dragon.desc: Wisdom, strength, protection and supernatural powers. Symbol of water and generosity.

carp.name: Koi Tattoo Munich
carp.desc: Endurance, courage and strength. The Koi tattoo in the Japanese Irezumi style — one of the most powerful motifs for a large-scale tattoo in Munich.

fox.name: Fox
fox.desc: Cunning, intellect, longevity and magical powers. Regarded as a protector against evil.

cherry.name: Cherry Blossom
cherry.desc: The transience and beauty of life — the philosophy of Memento Mori.

tiger.name: Tiger Tattoo Munich
tiger.desc: Strength, bravery and protection from illness. A Tiger tattoo in the Irezumi style — symbol of the wind and a classic of Japanese tattooing in Munich.

faq.heading: Frequently asked questions about the tattoo artist Munich
faq.label: FAQ
faq.question: Question №

item0.q: How much does a tattoo cost in Munich?
item0.a: Tattoo costs in Munich depend on size, style and complexity. Small tattoos from €150, a sleeve from €2,500 in total. A detailed overview can be found on the Tattoo Prices Munich page.

item1.q: Which tattoo styles does KishaTattoo Munich offer?
item1.a: We specialise in Japanese Irezumi (dragons, koi, kitsune, sakura), Fineline / Fine Line tattoo and graphic styles. All motifs are designed individually — no flash, no templates.

item2.q: How do I book an appointment at Kisha Tattoo Munich?
item2.a: Contact us via WhatsApp or Instagram DM with your idea, the desired body part and a preferred date. We will get back to you for a free consultation.

item3.q: How long does a large Irezumi tattoo take?
item3.a: Large-scale projects such as a sleeve or backpiece require 5–15+ sessions spaced 4–6 weeks apart for healing. Each session lasts 3–6 hours.

item4.q: How do I care for my new tattoo?
item4.a: Keep the tattoo clean, wash it gently with mild soap and apply a thin layer of moisturiser. Avoid sun, swimming and soaking for at least 2–3 weeks. All details can be found on our Aftercare page.

item0.desc: Botanical linework, geometric shapes, fine scripts — highest precision in the smallest space.

item1.desc: Koi, dragons, tigers — traditional compositions in the classic Irezumi visual language.

item2.desc: Custom graphic, blackwork, geometric tattoos — bold, timeless visual language.

Tattoo Artist Munich

hero.dedicated: Tattoo artist Munich — dedicated to preserving the Japanese tattoo tradition, one story at a time

journey.heading: The path: etched in discipline
journey.p1: For over five years I have dedicated myself as a tattoo artist in Munich to the Japanese tattoo tradition. My journey began with deep respect for the legacy of Irezumi — not as a trend, but as a commitment to a powerful, living art form.
journey.p2: As a tattooist in Munich I specialise in large-scale, complex work: full backpieces, sleeves and bodysuits. These projects require an exceptional degree of discipline and patience — qualities I bring to every single line.

philosophy.heading: The philosophy — love and devotion
philosophy.body: At KishaTattoo Munich we are guided by deep love for the craft and sincere dedication to your vision

mastery.heading: The commitment of the best tattooist in Munich
mastery.sub: Traditional Irezumi is an act of endurance — it demands patience from tattooist and client alike

steps.title: For the tattooist
steps.body: It requires focused discipline to master the technique, respect the anatomy of the body and dedicate countless hours to perfection.

steps.title: For the client
steps.body: The courage and patience to commit to a long process involving multiple sessions — knowing that the finished masterpiece will be a lasting testament to your inner strength.

steps.title: My dedication
steps.body: Goes to those who seek an authentic, meaningful and enduring work of art. As a tattoo artist in Munich I am here to guide the process and ensure that your legacy is beautifully etched into skin.

meta.title: FAQ — Kisha Tattoo Munich
meta.description: Frequently asked questions about appointments, prices, aftercare and tattoo styles at Kisha Tattoo Munich.

booking.q: How does booking at KishaTattoo work?
booking.a: Send a request via the booking form or Instagram DM with your idea, desired style, and placement. Kisha will get back to you within 48 hours for an initial consultation.

booking.q: How far in advance should I book?
booking.a: For smaller projects, appointments within 2–4 weeks are often available. Large projects like backpieces or sleeves are usually planned 1–3 months in advance. Just reach out — we'll find a solution.
Yes, a deposit is required for all projects. It will be deducted from the final price at your appointment. The amount is agreed individually during the consultation.
Rescheduling is possible with at least 72 hours notice. Cancellations under 48 hours may result in loss of deposit. We appreciate early communication.

pricing.q: What does a tattoo cost at KishaTattoo?
pricing.a: All projects are billed by the hour. Smaller designs start from approx. €150–200 (hourly rate + minimum fee), large projects like sleeves or backpieces from €800–1200 per session. You'll receive an exact quote after the consultation.

pricing.q: Why does a good tattoo cost so much?
pricing.a: A tattoo is for life. Custom design, precise technique, and quality materials take time and expertise. Cheap tattoos cost more in the end — in cover-ups, corrections, and rework.
Cash, bank transfer, and card payment. The deposit is made by bank transfer after the consultation. Cash payment on the day of the session is also accepted.

preparation.q: How do I prepare for a tattoo session?
preparation.a: Be well-rested, hydrated, and fed. Avoid alcohol and blood-thinning medications (aspirin, ibuprofen) 24 hours beforehand. Wear loose, comfortable clothing that provides easy access to the tattoo area.

preparation.q: Can I eat or drink during the session?
preparation.a: Absolutely! Bring snacks and drinks — especially during long sessions it's important to keep blood sugar stable and avoid circulation issues.

preparation.q: Can I bring someone with me?
preparation.a: One support person is welcome. For space reasons, please limit companions to one person.

preparation.q: I'm sick — should I still come?
preparation.a: No. If you have a cold, fever, or other infection, we'll reschedule. Your immune system needs all its resources for healing — we won't tattoo you.

aftercare.q: How do I care for a fresh tattoo?
aftercare.a: First 3–5 days: keep covered as instructed, gently clean with mild soapy water, apply a thin layer of recommended ointment (e.g. Bepanthen, Tattoo Goo). No sun, no swimming, no scratching. Full aftercare guide: /aftercare

aftercare.q: When is a tattoo fully healed?
aftercare.a: Surface healing takes 2–3 weeks. Full deep healing (dermal layer) takes 3–6 months. Only then can it be assessed whether touch-up work is needed.

aftercare.q: Can I exercise after getting a tattoo?
aftercare.a: Light activity is possible after 2–3 days. Intense sport, swimming pools, sauna, and direct sun only after full healing (minimum 2–3 weeks).

style.q: What's the difference between Irezumi and Japanese Style?
style.a: Traditional Irezumi follows centuries-old composition rules: motifs like koi, dragons, and oni are aligned to the body's musculature, with clearly defined transitions. 'Japanese Style' is often a freer, more modern interpretation.

style.q: How long does a large Irezumi project take?
style.a: A full back: 40–80 hours over 10–20 sessions spanning 1–3 years. A full sleeve: 20–40 hours over 6–18 months. Healing breaks between sessions are a fixed part of the process.

style.q: Does KishaTattoo work with client design ideas?
style.a: Absolutely — custom design is the standard, not a special service. Kisha develops each motif individually for your body shape, skin tone, and personal story. Reference images help to refine your vision.
Yes, cover-ups are possible. Feasibility depends on the size, darkness, and age of the existing tattoo. Send a photo with your enquiry — Kisha will assess the options.

cta.heading: Still have questions? Just reach out.

meta.title: Tattoo Aftercare — Kisha Tattoo Munich
meta.description: Aftercare instructions for your new tattoo from Kisha. Keep your Irezumi looking great for years.

hero.sub: A tattoo is for life — proper care determines the quality. Follow these instructions for optimal healing.

days.title: Right After the Session
steps: Keep the covering (film or bandage) on for 2–4 hours, as instructed by Kisha
Then gently wash with lukewarm water and mild, fragrance-free soap
Apply a thin layer of recommended ointment (e.g. Bepanthen, Tattoo Goo)

days.title: First Week
steps: Gently wash and apply a thin layer of ointment 2–3 times daily
The tattoo needs to breathe — no thick cream layers
No permanent plastic wrap — moisture causes maceration
Wear loose, soft clothing over the tattoo — no rubbing
Don't scratch or pick the scab — colour will be lost

days.title: Peeling & Healing
steps: The tattoo begins to peel — this is normal and part of healing
Continue moisturising when it feels dry
Still avoid UV exposure — no direct sun bathing
Continue to avoid swimming pools, saunas, and baths

items.label: Direct Sun
items.note: At least 3–4 weeks, then always SPF 50+

items.label: Swimming / Bathing
items.note: Pool, sea, bathtub — at least 3 weeks

items.label: Scratching & Rubbing
items.note: Removing scab removes pigment — be patient!

items.label: Intense Sport
items.note: Sweat and skin stretching: at least 1 week rest

items.label: Alcohol
items.note: 24h before and after the session — thins the blood

products.heading: Recommended Care Products

items.name: Tattoo Goo Original Salve
items.note: Made for tattoos, no fragrance additives

items.name: Hustle Butter Deluxe
items.note: Vegan, ideal for the first days and long-term care

products.disclaimer: * Kisha recommends specific products after the session — follow the personal instructions given.

school.heading:
Tattoo Training Munich —
a school that shapes masters
school.intro: Kisha is not just a tattoo artist — she is a trainer. Over the years she has trained more than 10 professional tattoo artists at her school in Munich, who now have established careers in Germany and Europe. Her training combines the discipline of Japanese Irezumi with modern craftsmanship.

stats.label: Trained Tattoo Artists
stats.body: Graduates of the school are now successful tattoo artists themselves in Germany and Europe.

stats.label: Years of Teaching Experience
stats.body: Kisha shares her knowledge in individual coaching and intensive training programmes in Munich.

stats.label: Practice-Based
